mechanism_narrative: Sustained month-over-month growth in nonfarm payrolls (PAYEMS) signals expanding labor income and consumer spending capacity, which supports corporate earnings expectations and drives S&P 500 forward returns higher over the next 20 trading days, independent of any threshold or regime condition.
failure_condition: Rank IC between PAYEMS 3-month trend and forward 20d SPX returns falls below 0.03 over any rolling 2-year walk-forward window, or positive PAYEMS trend periods show no statistically significant excess return versus unconditional baseline (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the effective federal funds rate rises more than 25 basis points over a trailing 3-month window, markets interpret this as the onset of a tightening cycle that raises the equity discount rate, causing S&P 500 forward returns to decline over the subsequent 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Triggered instances of FEDFUNDS 3-month rise > 0.25pp produce positive median forward 20d SPX return in more than 55% of cases across the walk-forward sample, or Rank IC < 0.03 in any 2-year subperiod.

mechanism_narrative: A joint decline in retail sales (RSAFS) and housing starts (HOUST) over a trailing 3-month window signals broad-based consumer and residential investment weakness that is not captured by either series alone, and this combined deterioration predicts negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days as earnings expectations reprice downward.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS 3-month change < 0 AND HOUST 3-month change < 0 simultaneously
failure_condition: Joint negative trigger periods fail to produce negative median forward 60d SPX return, or the interaction model's out-of-sample Rank IC does not exceed the sum of single-series IC contributions by a meaningful margin, indicating no true interaction effect.

mechanism_narrative: During a post-recession recovery regime, sustained month-over-month increases in industrial production (INDPRO) reflect rebuilding of manufacturing capacity and inventory restocking, which drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-trading-day horizon as forward earnings estimates are revised upward.
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO level 117.813 near post-2008 recovery trajectory
failure_condition: INDPRO uptrend periods within the recovery regime show forward 60d SPX returns not significantly different from zero (p > 0.05), or the effect reverses sign outside recession-recovery windows without being flagged as regime-conditional.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate (UNRATE) rises above a critical threshold indicating stalled labor market repair, risk-averse repricing of equities occurs, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Instances of UNRATE crossing above the 9.5% threshold are not followed by median negative forward 20d SPX returns, or hit rate of negative returns in triggered windows is statistically indistinguishable from the unconditional base rate (chi-square p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month acceleration in CPIAUCSL signals rising inflation expectations that compress near-term equity risk premia, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over a short 5-trading-day horizon as markets rapidly price in inflation surprise risk.
failure_condition: CPIAUCSL month-over-month acceleration shows no significant negative correlation with forward 5d SPX returns (Rank IC < 0.02) over any rolling 1-year window in the walk-forward evaluation.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth (GDPC1) combined with concurrent retail sales (RSAFS) growth jointly confirms genuine broad-based demand expansion rather than a statistical GDP anomaly, and this confirmed expansion predicts positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days; GDPC1 growth alone without corroborating RSAFS growth is insufficient signal.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 quarter-over-quarter growth > 0 AND RSAFS 3-month growth > 0
failure_condition: Periods satisfying both GDPC1 and RSAFS positive growth conditions fail to show forward 20d SPX returns exceeding the unconditional mean, or the joint-condition Rank IC is not measurably higher than either single-series IC, indicating no true interaction benefit.

mechanism_narrative: In a jobless-recovery regime where payroll growth (PAYEMS) resumes but unemployment (UNRATE) remains elevated or rising, the divergence signals labor slack that caps consumer spending upside, causing the typical positive payroll-growth equity response to weaken or reverse over a 60-trading-day horizon compared to a normal recovery regime.
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE elevated at 5.2 with modest PAYEMS level 132573 in snapshot
regime_hint: jobless recovery with elevated structural unemployment
failure_condition: Forward 60d SPX returns during PAYEMS-growth-with-elevated-UNRATE periods are not significantly lower than forward returns during PAYEMS-growth-with-declining-UNRATE periods (difference-in-means p > 0.05), invalidating the regime-conditional divergence claim.
